title: Worsening cough and breathlessness in a smoker

chart.oneLiner: 64F with a long smoking history and known COPD presents with 3 days of worsening dyspnea, increased cough, and a change to purulent sputum.

opening: My breathing's gotten much worse the last few days, and I'm coughing up a lot more gunk than usual.
patientFile:
PERSONA: 64-year-old woman, pursed-lip breathing, sitting forward, speaking in short phrases. Cooperative.

HPI: Over 3 days, dyspnea worse than her usual baseline, increased cough with a change from clear to thick yellow-green sputum, and more wheeze. Had a head cold last week. Uses her inhalers more often with less relief. NO chest pain, NO orthopnea/PND, NO leg swelling, NO fever chills beyond feeling warm, NO hemoptysis, NO calf pain/travel.
BASELINE: known COPD, chronic morning cough, gets short of breath walking one block at baseline.
PMH: COPD, hypertension. MEDS: tiotropium, albuterol PRN, lisinopril. Generally adherent.
SOCIAL: current smoker, ~45 pack-years. Lives with spouse.

PHYSICAL EXAM (report ONLY what is requested; give normals when asked):
- General: mild-moderate distress, accessory muscle use, pursed-lip breathing. Barrel chest.
- Vitals: BP 138/84, HR 104, RR 26, 37.8°C, SpO2 88% RA.
- Lungs: diffuse expiratory WHEEZE, prolonged expiratory phase, globally decreased breath sounds, hyperresonant to percussion. NO focal consolidation.
- Cardiac: tachycardic, regular, distant heart sounds, no murmur.
- Neck: JVP NOT elevated. Extremities: NO pedal edema, no calf tenderness.

HIDDEN RULES:
- NEVER state or hint at a diagnosis. Answer only what is asked.
- Do NOT volunteer the smoking amount or the recent cold unless asked about smoking or recent illness.
- The absent JVD/edema are revealed only if those are examined (they help distinguish from heart failure).
- If asked something not in this file, give a reasonable normal/negative answer.

labs.ABG: pH 7.34, PaCO2 55, PaO2 58, HCO3 30 — chronic respiratory acidosis with metabolic compensation, acute-on-chronic.
labs.BNP: 85 pg/mL — not elevated (argues against heart failure).

ekg.label: 12-lead EKG
ekg.findings: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes.
ekg.verify: From the LITFL 'ECG in Chronic Lung Disease (COPD)' library page — download an example tracing that matches: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes. … and self-host with credit to litfl.com.
ekg.attribution: LITFL ECG Library (litfl.com) — © Life in the Fast Lane, CC BY-NC-SA 4.0. Educational/not-for-profit use, credit litfl.com; skip any image labelled third-party.
ekg.imageDescription: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes.

cxr.label: Chest X-ray (PA)
cxr.findings: Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter/retrosternal air, no focal consolidation or edema.
cxr.verify: Pick a CC file showing emphysematous hyperinflation (flattened diaphragms), NOT a lobar infiltrate or CHF pattern.
cxr.imageDescription: Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter/retrosternal air, no focal consolidation or edema.
searchTerms: chest radiograph Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms

steps.label: Differential
steps.prompt: Based on your history and physical, list your differential diagnosis (note your leading diagnosis).
steps.rubric: COPD exacerbation (leading,7; accepts AECOPD/COPD flare). Pneumonia (core,4). Pulmonary embolism (CANT-MISS,5). Acute coronary syndrome (CANT-MISS,4). Heart failure (core,3). Named leading dx w/ justification (core,2).
steps.idealAnswer: Leading diagnosis: COPD exacerbation. Must-not-miss diagnoses to exclude: Pulmonary embolism; Acute coronary syndrome. Also reasonable: Pneumonia; Heart failure.

steps.label: Workup
steps.prompt: What initial diagnostic workup do you want to order?
steps.rubric: CXR (CANT-MISS,4). ABG / VBG (core,3). CBC (core,2). BMP (core,2). EKG (core,2). BNP to help exclude heart failure (core,2). PENALTY: shotgun panel w/ no reasoning (-2).
steps.idealAnswer: Order: CXR; ABG / VBG; CBC; BMP; EKG; BNP to help exclude heart failure.

coreActions.item: BNP to help exclude heart failure

optionalTests: BNP to help exclude heart failure

steps.label: Read EKG
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's 12-lead EKG. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = sinus tachycardia, possible P pulmonale/low voltage, NO acute ischemia. CREDIT: rate & rhythm (sinus tachy) (6); recognizes no acute ischemic ST changes (5); notes any right-heart/COPD features e.g. P pulmonale (2); systematic read (2). PENALTY: over-calling acute ischemia (-3).
keyFindings: recognizes no acute ischemic ST changes
notes any right-heart/COPD features e.g. P pulmonale
criticalFindings: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes.
steps.idealAnswer: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes.

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Sinus tachycardia. Possible P pulmonale / low voltage. No acute ischemic ST changes.

coreActions.item: recognizes no acute ischemic ST changes
notes any right-heart/COPD features e.g. P pulmonale

steps.label: Read CXR
steps.prompt: Here is the patient's chest X-ray. Describe your findings.
steps.rubric: Findings = hyperinflation, flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter, NO focal consolidation or pulmonary edema. CREDIT: hyperinflation/flattened diaphragms (5); increased lucency/AP diameter (2); correctly notes NO consolidation or congestion (3). PENALTY: calling a pneumonia or CHF that isn't there (-2).
keyFindings: correctly notes NO consolidation or congestion
criticalFindings: Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter/retrosternal air, no focal consolidation or edema.
commonMistakes: calling a pneumonia or CHF that isn't there
steps.idealAnswer: Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter/retrosternal air, no focal consolidation or edema.

criticalActions.item: Recognizes: Hyperinflation with flattened diaphragms, increased AP diameter/retrosternal air, no focal consolidation or edema.

coreActions.item: correctly notes NO consolidation or congestion

penalties.item: calling a pneumonia or CHF that isn't there

steps.label: Revised Dx
steps.prompt: Pulling the data together, what is your leading diagnosis and reasoning?
steps.rubric: COPD exacerbation (5). Uses the normal BNP / absent JVD-edema to argue against heart failure and the clear film to argue against pneumonia (3). Notes acute-on-chronic respiratory acidosis on ABG (bonus,2).

bonusActions.item: Notes acute-on-chronic respiratory acidosis on ABG

steps.label: Management
steps.rubric: CONTROLLED supplemental O2 targeting SpO2 88–92% (CANT-MISS,6). Inhaled bronchodilators — SABA + SAMA (6). Systemic corticosteroids (5). Antibiotics given purulent sputum / increased dyspnea (4). Consider non-invasive ventilation for respiratory acidosis (3). Smoking cessation counseling (bonus,1). PENALTY: high-flow/100% O2 risking CO2 narcosis (-4).
steps.idealAnswer: Plan: CONTROLLED supplemental O2 targeting SpO2 88–92%.

caseSummary.diagnosticFramework: 64F with a long smoking history and known COPD presents with 3 days of worsening dyspnea, increased cough, and a change to purulent sputum. The diagnosis is COPD exacerbation, reached by eliciting the focused history, performing the targeted exam, and interpreting the directed workup while excluding the must-not-miss alternatives.
keyPhysicalExamFindings: Cardiac: tachycardic, regular, distant heart sounds, no murmur.
Neck: JVP NOT elevated. Extremities: NO pedal edema, no calf tenderness.
Lungs: diffuse expiratory WHEEZE, prolonged expiratory phase, globally decreased breath sounds, hyperresonant to percussion. NO focal consolidation.
Lungs: diffuse expiratory WHEEZE, prolonged expiratory phase, globally decreased breath sounds, hyperresonant to percussion. NO focal consolidation.

reasoningPathway: Frame the presentation from the chart and vitals.
Elicit the focused history, including sensitive items only on direct questioning.
Perform the targeted physical exam maneuvers to reveal key findings.
Build a differential led by COPD exacerbation while explicitly excluding must-not-miss causes.
Order a focused, order-sensitive workup and interpret EKG/CXR and labs.
Commit to COPD exacerbation and manage with stabilization, definitive treatment, consults, and disposition.

imageInterpretationPearls: An incidental abnormality is an interpretation task in its own right — name it, then judge its relevance.
Read every study systematically before committing.
State pertinent negatives, not just positives.
